approach: Spec-only re-altitude CR, distilled from a ratified Scanner strategy candidate (strategy 109cb2, seq 5, worktree-provision). The six provision scenarios in mux/worktree/worktree.feature narrated a `cyber-mux worktree provision` CLI verb that does not exist: cli.ts wires only worktree add/open/list/remove/prune, and provision ships ONLY as the library seam provisionWorktree / WorktreeApi.provision (src/worktree.ts). The worktree-provision ADR itself defers a CLI verb as an unshipped follow-up ('Left as a clean follow-up if a CLI worktree provision verb ever wants one'). So the frozen suite asserted a surface the CLI never reaches — a spec-feature-contradiction the impl gate missed. Fix A (re-altitude the Givens to the shipped seam) over fix B (build the verb), per the ADR/Council intent.

Blast radius small and spec-only: 4 Given clauses re-altituded plus one band comment in worktree.feature; scenarios 5-6 opened on a predicate and were already surface-neutral. No source change is proposed — the already-shipped provisionWorktree seam is the subject, not the target. Every When/Then acceptance line is byte-identical pre/post-edit (verified by git diff and classify-edit-class). Compatibility does not fire (package is 0.0.0, nothing shipped).

Edit class is a NARROWING/rewrite of frozen scenario Givens (classify-edit-class.mts: 4 modified, 0 added, 0 removed), so it is a genuine re-open of worktree.feature, NOT a self-clearing amendment. An initial 'obvious stale-mistake, conductor-served, no re-open' framing was rejected by the cold spec-judge and dropped: the trichotomy (additive self-clears / pure-move preserves / narrowing-or-rewrite re-opens) has no fourth bucket, and 'obviously fine' is the exact self-assessment Clearance exists to prevent. The re-open is instead RATIFIED by the mission mandate — the doctrine Scanner's ratified strategy candidate plus the operator brief explicitly chartering the frozen-Given re-altitude — which is the lightweight async re-open flag. Clearance is pre-authorizable in the CR and is pre-authorized by that mandate; gradient low/low/high, so self-assert within the auto-spec leash rather than stop.

why.floor: Clearance fires and CLEARS — it does not stop. classify-edit-class.mts reports NARROWING (4 modified provision Givens, 0 added, 0 removed), so worktree.feature genuinely re-opens; this is not an additive self-clear. The re-open is ratified by the mission mandate (doctrine Scanner strategy candidate 109cb2 seq 5 + the operator brief chartering the re-altitude) — the async re-open flag — and Clearance is pre-authorized in that CR. Clearance is nonetheless a real narrowing check, run explicitly: no acceptance scenario is weakened or deleted — every When/Then is byte-identical to pre-edit, only the Given surface descriptor changed from a nonexistent CLI verb to the shipped provisionWorktree / WorktreeApi.provision seam. Compatibility does not fire (package 0.0.0). No Conflict: the ADR settles provision as library-only, so the CLI narration was a factual error with only one plausibly-intended side.
why.blast: low — spec-only, one file: 4 Given clauses re-altituded plus one band comment in worktree.feature. No source change (the shipped seam is the subject, not the target); scenarios 5-6 were already surface-neutral and untouched; mux/worktree/README.md prose was already surface-neutral (no CLI provision claim) and needed no edit.
why.novelty: low — no new behavior specified. The change corrects which surface the existing, already-implemented provision behavior is attributed to; the behavioral contract (reuse/reset-pristine/create/gate/predicate/primary-checkout) is unchanged.
why.confidence: high — cold spec-judge at depth 1, re-deriving its own oracle, returned PASS on all three lenses (oracle: the re-altituded Givens name a surface that genuinely ships, independently confirmed against cli.ts/worktree.ts/the ADR; builder: the Givens are concrete and buildable against the real seam; architect: right altitude, the ADR's own deferral language confirms fix A over fix B). Round 1 caught a real producer under-declaration (governances_loaded omitted the oracle-spec/builder-spec bars) — refiled and passed. Round 2 caught my edit-class mishandling (stale-mistake self-clear framing) and forced the correct re-open+Clearance path recorded here. A non-blocking Given-uniformity nit (scenario 1 alone carried a parenthetical alias) was fixed. Mechanical checks green: check:features parses all 7 features; classify-edit-class confirms Given-only modification. Self-asserted within the auto-spec leash; lands provisionally in the async review queue: ratify or kick back.

why.floor: none at the impl gate. This CR proposes no source change; the provisionWorktree / WorktreeApi.provision seam is already shipped and was impl-gate-verified under the original provision CR (issue #79 / worktree-provision). Because every re-altituded scenario's When/Then acceptance is byte-identical to that already-verified contract, impl-sync is unchanged — the same tests still cover the same behavior.
why.blast: small — no source change. The behavior under verification is the already-shipped provisionWorktree seam in src/worktree.ts; only the spec's surface descriptor changed.
why.novelty: low — verification only, over an already-green suite.
why.confidence: high — the frozen provision scenarios' acceptance is verified by 8 passing provision tests in src/worktree.test.ts (reuse-and-reset-pristine, explicit base, create-when-none, never-reuse-unmerged-under-default-gate, skip-predicate-excluded, never-reuse-primary-checkout), run green (`vitest run src -t provision`: 8 passed). No dedicated cold impl-judge was dispatched: this is a spec-only re-altitude whose subject is an already-verified implementation and whose acceptance lines did not change, mirroring the #63 precedent; self-asserted within the auto-spec leash.

note: Root-cause hardening (cross-repo, cyberplace/plugins/sdd, not this repo): suite-format-governance does not require a scenario's Given to name a surface that actually ships, which is how these six provision scenarios froze narrating a `cyber-mux worktree provision` CLI verb the impl never wired. A suite-format bar — a Given must name a shipped surface (a CLI verb that exists, or a library seam), and the spec-judge's builder lens should catch a Given naming a nonexistent surface — would have blocked this at the original freeze. Filing is forge-conditional and cross-repo: not filed here (no confirmed permission, and the cyber-mux issue tracker is the wrong home for a cyberplace governance change); surfaced to the operator instead.

STRUCTURAL finding (supersedes the narrower seq-4 validity gap): capability-first SDD specs carry only a capability axis, but a project can expose one capability through multiple public surfaces (here CLI + library API) that DIVERGE in what they offer — provision ships on the API, not the CLI; open/prune are CLI-only. One capability-keyed suite cannot represent per-(capability x surface) exposure: picking a surface hides the divergence, abstracting over surface asserts a capability the missing surface lacks. Per-surface exposure is itself a behavioral contract an abstract suite deletes. Filed to cyberplace as a spec-structure / suite-format governance concern at operator request; the seq-4 mechanical backstop (a Given must name a shipped surface) folds into it.
